en: I didn't have to because of Jeremy Baramy.
ko: 제러미 바라미 덕분에 그럴 필요가 없었어.

en: Who's Jeremy Baramy?
Okay.
Things in the afterlife don't happen
while things are happening here
ko: 여기서 일어나는 일들과 동시에 일어나지 않아.

en: because while time on earth moves in a straight line,
one thing happens, then the next, then the next.
ko: 하나의 일이 일어나고, 그 다음 일이 일어나고, 또 그 다음 일이 일어나.

en: Time in the afterlife moves in a Jeremy Baramy.
What?
In the afterlife, time doubles back and loops around
and ends up looking something like Jeremy Baramy.
This is the timeline in the afterlife.
Happens to kind of look like the name Jeremy Baramy
ko: 영어 필기체로 제러미 바라미라는 이름처럼 보이기도 해, 그래서 그렇게 부르는 거야.

en: in cursive English, so that's what we call it.
Sorry, my brain is melting.
How can events happen before the ones that happened before?
ko: 어떻게 사건이 이전 사건보다 먼저 일어날 수 있어?

en: Just the way it was.
It's Jeremy Baramy.
I don't know what to tell you.
That's the easiest way to describe it. Okay, but...
